content.problemStatement:
Chef is a cook and he has recently opened a restaurant.

The restaurant is open during NNN time intervals [L1,R1),[L2,R2),…,[LN,RN)[L_1, R_1), [L_2, R_2), \dots, [L_N, R_N)[L1​,R1​),[L2​,R2​),…,[LN​,RN​). No two of these intervals overlap — formally, for each valid iii, jjj such that i≠ji \neq ji=j, either Ri<LjR_i \lt L_jRi​<Lj​ or Rj<LiR_j \lt L_iRj​<Li​.

MMM people (numbered 111 through MMM) are planning to eat at the restaurant; let's denote the time when the iii-th person arrives by PiP_iPi​. If the restaurant is open at that time, this person does not have to wait, but if it is closed, this person will wait until it is open. Note that if this person arrives at an exact time when the restaurant is closing, they have to wait for the next opening time.

For each person, calculate how long they have to wait (possibly 000 time), or determine that they will wait forever for the restaurant to open.
constraints: 1≤Li<Ri≤1091 \le L_i \lt R_i \le 10^91≤Li​<Ri​≤109 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
the sum of MMM for all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
the sum of MMM for all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a single integer PiP_iPi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print MMM lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain a single integer — the time person iii has to wait, or −1-1−1 if person iii has to wait forever.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 111: The person coming at time 555 does not have to wait as the restaurant is open in [5,7)[5,7)[5,7) slot.
Query 222: The person coming at time 666 does not have to wait as the restaurant is open in [5,7)[5,7)[5,7) slot.
Query 333: The person coming at time 777 has to wait since the restaurant is closed at time 777. The next slot in which the restaurant opens is [9,10)[9,10)[9,10). Thus, the person waits for 222 units of time.
Query 444: The person coming at time 353535 has to wait forever as the restaurant does not open after time 303030.
